text:
An old oak tree stood steadfast on a farm, its gnarled branches weaving tales of yore. Winter's silence was broken one crisp morning by a gentle chirrup; a cricket whispered about an unlikely hero.

A young owl chick was scared away from home. Every night, lonely and fearful, it'd glide over thosewoods. One starspirial eve,OWLowl fluttered past the oak. With Owlowl's keen eyes, it saw something glimmering on a branch—a bird's feather.

With courage Owlowl didn't know it possessed, it swooped down eager for redemption. It whispered soothing words, secrets only owls knew. The bird trusted Owlowl.

They soared together through a peaceful dusk. Night fell early. Owlowl perched atop the oldest branch owl ever earfinned.

"Why?" the feathered guest asked.

"To talk forever."

So they did. Owlowl listened; the old owl listened good.

When daylight came, Owlowl flew away. But promises
